fields.name: maximum soft landing time:seconds#the longest amount of time the biped can take to recover from a soft landing
maximum hard landing time:seconds#the longest amount of time the biped can take to recover from a hard landing
minimum soft landing velocity:world units per second#below this velocity the biped does not react when landing
minimum hard landing velocity:world units per second#below this velocity the biped will not do a soft landing when returning to the ground
maximum hard landing velocity:world units per second#the velocity corresponding to the maximum landing time
death hard landing velocity:world units per second#the maximum velocity with which a character can strike the ground and live
stun duration#0 is the default.  Bipeds are stuned when damaged by vehicle collisions, also some are when they take emp damage
camera interpolation start:degrees#looking-downward angle that starts camera interpolation to fp position
camera interpolation end:degrees#looking-downward angle at which camera interpolation to fp position is complete
camera forward movement scale#amount of fp camera movement forward and back (1.0 is full)
camera side movement scale#amount of fp camera movement side-to-side (1.0 is full)
camera vertical movement scale#amount of fp camera movement vertically (1.0 is full)
camera exclusion distance:world units#fp camera must always be at least this far out from root node
head shot acc scale#when the biped ragdolls from a head shot it acceleartes based on this value.  0 defaults to the standard acceleration scale
contact points#these are the points where the biped touches the ground
reanimation character#when the flood reanimate this guy, he turns into a ...
reanimation/morph muffins#the kind of muffins I create to cover my horrible transformation
death spawn character#when I die, out of the ashes of my death crawls a ...
distance of dive anim:world units#this must be set to tell the AI how far it should expect our dive animation to move us
spawned turret character#automatically created character when this unit is driven
aiming acceleration maximum:degrees per second squared
looking acceleration maximum:degrees per second squared
right_hand_node#where the primary weapon is attached
left_hand_node#where the secondary weapon is attached (for dual-pistol modes)
acceleration scale:[0,+inf]#marine 1.0, grunt 1.4, elite 0.9, hunter 0.5, etc.
dynamic light sphere radius#sphere to use for dynamic lights and shadows. only used if not 0
dynamic light sphere offset#only used if radius not 0
only set this tag if you want to override the default collision damage values in globals.globals
melee sound#this is the sound that is made when I am meleed.  This overrides the sweetener sound of my material.
ai type name#used for combat dialogue, etc.
turn off with#if the specified function is off, so is this function
min value#function must exceed this value (after mapping) to be active 0. means do nothing
variant name#if empty, may be used by any model variant
GAME ENGINE INCLUSION
fields.definition: Set which game engines you desire this object to appear in

fields.name: TYPE AND FLAGS
fields.definition:
Type pertains to how you want the game engine to handle this object
Certain flags applicable only to certain object types, should be self evident

fields.name: GOAL & RESPAWN ZONE BOUNDARY DATA
fields.definition: These fields are only used for goal area objects with boundaries, and for respawn zones

fields.name: SPAWNING DATA
fields.definition: These fields are used for default spawning times and remapping

fields.name: MORE GOAL & RESPAWN ZONE OBJECT BOUNDARY
fields.definition: These fields are only used for goal area objects with boundaries, and for respawn zones

fields.name: RESPAWN ZONE DATA
fields.definition: These are respawn zone weights, used only for respawn zones

fields.name: MARKER DATA
fields.definition: These fields are only used for defining certain, special markers to use for positional locations if the default position (object origin) is not sufficient

fields.name: SPAWNED OBJECT
fields.definition: If this object is intended to spawn something, the object it spawns should be specified here

fields.name: BOUNDARY SHADER - DEFAULT
fields.definition: These are default shaders used for boundary geometry

fields.name: BOUNDARY SHADER - SPHERE
fields.definition: These are custom shaders used for spherical boundary geometry

fields.name: BOUNDARY SHADER - CYLINDER
fields.definition: These are custom shaders used for cylindrical boundary geometry

fields.name: BOUNDARY SHADER - BOX
fields.definition: These are custom shaders used for box boundary geometry

fields.name: camera scale x MIN(scale x POWER((K x X), power), maximum)
camera scale (axial)#scale factor used when this acceleration component is along the axis of the forward vector of the camera
camera scale (perpendicular)#scale factor used when this acceleration component is perpendicular to the camera
acceleration range:world units per second squared
preferred_gun_node#if found, use this gun marker
ping scale#nathan is too lazy to make pings for each seat.
turnover time:seconds#how much time it takes to evict a rider from a flipped vehicle
listener interpolation factor#how far to interpolate listener position from camera to occupant's head
speed dependant turn rates
fields.definition:
when the unit velocity is 0, the yaw/pitch rates are the left values
at [max speed reference], the yaw/pitch rates are the right values.
the max speed reference is what the code uses to generate a clamped speed from 0..1
the exponent controls how midrange speeds are interpreted.

fields.name: pitch interpolation time:seconds to interpolate#0 means use default 17
entry fields
fields.definition:
note: the entry radius shouldn't exceed 3 world units, 
as that is as far as the player will search for a vehicle
to enter.

fields.name: entry radius#how close to the entry marker a unit must be
entry marker cone angle#angle from marker forward the unit must be
entry marker facing angle#angle from unit facing the marker must be
living material name#collision material used when character is alive
dead material name#collision material used when character is dead
dead sphere shapes*#don't be a jerk, edit something else!
pill shapes*#don't be a jerk, edit something else!
sphere shapes*#don't be a jerk, edit something else!
marker name#you don't need this if you're just generating effects.  If empty it defaults to the up of the object
alignment marker name#you don't need this if you're just generating effects.  If empty it defaults to "marker name"
Linear Motion
fields.definition:
0 - means do nothing
CENTER: motion towards marker position 
AXIS: motion towards marker axis, such that object is on the axis
DIRECTION: motion along marker direction

fields.name: hookes law e#0 if you don't want this to behave like spring.  1 is a good starting point if you do.
linear dead radius#radius from linear motion origin in which acceleration is dead.
Angular Motion
fields.definition:
0 - means do nothing
ALIGNMENT: algin objects in the phantom with the marker
SPIN: spin the object about the marker axis

fields.name: alignment hookes law e#0 if you don't want this to behave like spring.  1 is a good starting point if you do.
climb inflection angle#angle for bipeds at which climb direction changes between up and down
scale airborne reaction time#scale on the time for the entity to realize it is airborne
scale ground adhesion velocity#scale on velocity with which the entity is pushed back into its ground plane
gravity scale#scale on gravity for this entity
bank angle:degrees#angle at which we bank left/right when sidestepping or turning while moving forwards
bank apply time:seconds#time it takes us to apply a bank
bank decay time:seconds#time it takes us to recover from a bank
pitch ratio#amount that we pitch up/down when moving up or down
max velocity:world units per second#max velocity when not crouching
max sidestep velocity:world units per second#max sideways or up/down velocity when not crouching
angular velocity maximum:degrees per second#turn rate
angular acceleration maximum:degrees per second squared#turn acceleration rate
crouch velocity modifier:[0,1]#how much slower we fly if crouching (zero = same speed)
roll delay:[0,1] 1= roll fast and late
lobbing desire:1= heavy arc, 0= no arc
ground normal dampening:react to slope changes (0=slow, 1= fast)
root offset max scale:vertical drop to ground allowed (0=none, 1=full)
root offset dampening:react to root changes (0=slow, 1= fast)
following cam scale:root offset effect on following cam (0=none, 1=full)
foot roll max:orient to ground slope (degrees)
foot pitch max:orient to ground slope (degrees)
pivot min foot delta: vert world units to find lowest foot
pivot stride length scale: leg length * this = stride length
pivot throttle scale:pivoting slows throttle (0=none, 1= full)
pivot offset dampening:react to pivot changes (0=slow, 1= fast)

options: search cardinal direction lightmaps on failure
extension of parent#object passes all function values to parent and uses parent's markers
attach to clusters by dynamic sphere#use this for the mac gun on spacestation
effects created by this object do not spawn objects in multiplayer
does not collide with camera#specificly the flying observer camera
damage not blocked by obstructions#AOE damage being applied to this object does not test for obstrutions.
invert#result of function is one minus actual result
mapping does not controls active#the curve mapping can make the function active/inactive
always active#function does not deactivate when at or below lower bound
random time offset#function offsets periodic function input by random value between 0 and 1
always exports value#when this function is deactivated, it still exports its value
turn off with uses magnitude#the function will be turned off if the value of the turns_off_with function is 0
blend in hsv#blends colors in hsv rather than rgb space
...more colors#blends colors through more hues (goes the long way around the color wheel)
flashlight power doesnt transfer to weapon
allow aim while opening or closing
first person camera slaved to gun.
ai firing disabled by max acceleration
